'''Kyli Ned'Ix''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] adventurer and [[Technician/Legends|technician]]. Able to repair or modify almost any type of equipment, Ned'Ix found himself on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Following the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of the world by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Ned'Ix attempted to join the crew of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which was tasked with a mission to [[DarkStryder Campaign|track down]] the rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], who had fled from Kal'Shebbol with his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. Despite Ned'Ix's superior technical skills, the command crew of the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] felt that his habit of jury-rigging equipment was actually more of a danger than an asset and turned him down. Undaunted, Ned'Ix repaired and modified a [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc Industries]] [[720 light freighter/Legends|Class 720 freighter]] that he found abandoned on the planet. Naming it the "''[[Fxz'Et]]''," he loaded the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] with spare parts and junk and headed out after the ''FarStar'', determined to help the expedition in any way he could.

==Biography==
Kyli Ned'Ix, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]], was a master [[Technician/Legends|technician]] who could fix or modify nearly any piece of equipment, and was able to craft small but useful gadgets from junk, although he refrained from fashioning weapons of any kind. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Ned'Ix was present on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="tdc" /> when it was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberated]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]]. The local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], was forced to flee his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] and headed deeper into the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] with the bulk of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|military forces]]. Realizing the threat that he still presented to the stability of the sector, the New Republic authorized a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to track him down, assigning the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to the job. However, a lack of available manpower meant that the New Republic was forced to hire civilian personnel from the local population of Kal'Shebbol to assist in crewing the vessel.<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Ned'Ix learned of the mission, and, seeing an opportunity for adventure, he applied to join the crew. However, despite his exceptional technical skills, Ned'Ix was turned down as the command crew felt that his penchant for jury-rigging and modifying equipment could have placed the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and crew in unnecessary danger.<ref name="tdc" />

[[File:FxzEt-DSC.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Fxz'Et'']]
Rather than be left on Kal'Shebbol, Ned'Ix resolved to help the ''FarStar'' in any way possible. Discovering an old [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc Industries]] [[720 light freighter/Legends|Class 720 freighter]] that had been abandoned on Kal'Shebbol, he repaired the vessel and enhanced it with junk components left behind by both the New Republic and the Galactic Empire. Ned'Ix gathered together a large amount of technological parts—including disassembled [[Droid/Legends|droids]], fused [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] circuit boards, and [[Ion engine/Legends|ion drive]] [[Power coupling/Legends|power couplings]]—and fitted the ship with a small [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] and a pair of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|launchers]]. Naming the vessel the "''[[Fxz'Et]]''," Ned'Ix launched after the ''FarStar'', determined to aid the New Republic crew with their mission, even if they didn't want him aboard the ship.<ref name="tdc" />

{{RPGseed}}
Ned'Ix encountered the crew of the ''FarStar'' on several occasions during their [[Standard month/Legends|months]]-long mission. However, he was still not allowed to join the crew despite his wishes, settling instead for a little recognition for his efforts when he was of service to them, and assistance from them with navigating his way through the Kathol sector.<ref name="tdc" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Kyli Ned'Ix's [[Chitin/Legends|chitinous]] carapace was [[Color/Legends|green]], and like all Verpine, gave him a measure of protection against physical blows. His [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were the same color as his carapace, and he possessed the Verpine's [[Microscope/Legends|microscopic sight]]. Ned'Ix was capable of communicating with other Verpine, and even specially tuned [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]], within a range of one [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] by using his antenna as an organic telecommunicator. A determined and adventurous being, Ned'Ix wanting nothing more than to join the crew of the ''FarStar'' and assist them in their expedition. Even when told that he could not join the crew, he did not hold a grudge and came up with a way to assist them in his own fashion.<ref name="tdc" />

A highly skilled technician, Ned'Ix could repair or modify most equipment, and loved creating new devices from the junk in the hold of the ''Fxz'Et''. His skills allowed him to modify a comlink into a [[Jammer/Legends|jamming]] device, but he was easily distracted when working on a new gadget. However, he indulged his creativity too much sometimes, often to the point of forgetting about everything else. Several [[Time/Legends|times]], Ned'Ix landed the ''Fxz'Et'' on the nearest planet he could find regardless of any danger, or allowed the ship to drift in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. Often, the result was Ned'Ix getting into trouble with local Imperial forces or criminal elements who discovered him. He also had a habit of modifying equipment that did not need it. This usually resulted in making the equipment more powerful or more useful, but rendering it unstable at the same time.<ref name="tdc" />

A capable [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogator]], Ned'Ix could fly small vessels and operate their weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] systems effectively. His technical expertise allowed him to repair [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[Computer/Legends|computers]], droids, space [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and he was able to program both computers and droids. He was skilled in the use of explosives and security systems. Ned'Ix typically carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] for defense, along with his comlink jammer, a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and [[security cardlock breaker]].<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kyli Ned'Ix was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]]. Appearing only in the Rogues Gallery, the character was intended as both a potential source of comic relief for the adventure by blundering into a delicate situation at the wrong time, and as a gamemaster "out;" Ned'Ix could be used to rescue players who were having trouble in an adventure by supplying them with a helpful gadget, or by leading them to where the Gamemaster intended them to go. He was mentioned specifically in the adventure ''[[Shintel Downtime]]''; during the adventure, the ''FarStar'' is attacked by [[Qulok's Fist]], a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] working for Moff Sarne. If the players are having trouble dealing with their opponents, one of the suggested options that the gamemaster can employ to help the characters is that Ned'Ix arrives in the [[Shintel system/Legends|system]] and assists them.

Ned'Ix was later namechecked in [[2010]]'s ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'', a sourcebook for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]], as an example name for Verpine characters.
